choose.title: Welcome to Openship
choose.subtitle: Choose how you'd like to run Openship. Both options give you every feature - and you can switch anytime.

cloud.name: Openship Cloud
cloud.desc: We handle infrastructure, updates, and backups. Just sign in and start deploying.

selfhost.name: Self-Hosted
selfhost.desc: Run Openship on your own hardware. Connect a remote server or use this machine.

choose.migrateNote: Switch between Cloud and Self-Hosted anytime with one click - no lock-in, ever.

selfhost.title: Where should Openship run?
selfhost.subtitleLine1: Pick where to install the Openship platform.
selfhost.subtitleLine2: Both options give you full control and data ownership.

local.title: This Machine
local.desc: Install Openship right here - no SSH, no remote setup needed
local.comingSoonDesc: Running Openship directly on this machine isn't ready yet — connect a server for now.

remote.title: Another Server
remote.desc: Connect to a remote server, VPS, or any machine on your network via SSH

ssh.title: Connect to your server
ssh.subtitleLine1: Enter your server details and choose an auth method.
ssh.subtitleLine2: We'll install and configure everything automatically.
ssh.agentHint: Connects using this machine's SSH agent — like VSCode, no password or key needed. The host running Openship must already have access to this server (a key loaded in its ssh-agent).
ssh.testHint: Verify the server is reachable before continuing.

tunnel.title: Internet Access
tunnel.subtitle: Your server needs a way to be reachable from the internet for webhooks, SSL, and public access.

edge.title: Openship Edge
edge.desc: Zero config. Automatic SSL, high availability, and DDoS protection - all built in.

cloudflare.title: Cloudflare Tunnel
cloudflare.desc: Use your own Cloudflare account. Free tier available.

ngrok.title: ngrok
ngrok.desc: Expose services with your ngrok auth token.

tunnel.edgeHint: Openship Edge requires an Openship account. You'll sign in to continue.

preferences.title: Build Preferences
preferences.subtitleLine1: Choose how your projects are built when deploying.
preferences.subtitleLine2: You can change this later in settings.

auto.title: Auto
auto.desc: We pick the best option per framework

server.title: Remote
server.desc: Build on the deployment server directly

local.title: Local
local.desc: Build on this Mac, then push to the server

preferences.hint: Git push deployments always build on the server - this only applies to manual deploys.

loading.couldNotStartAuthMsg: The desktop auth flow could not be started.
loading.authFailedMsg: Please try again and finish sign-in in your browser.
loading.newTabSignIn: You'll complete sign-in in a new tab
loading.couldNotSaveMsg: The API didn't respond. Make sure services are running.
